preamble: The transformation of a voiced velar consonant /g/ into a voiced labiodental fricative /v/, typically through a labiovelar stage or spirantization.

examples.note: In Zan languages (Mingrelian, Laz), some Proto-Kartvelian *g reflexes appear as [v] through spirantization. E.g., PK *mgel- (wolf) → Mingrelian mgveri (showing g→gv/v alternation in certain positions).
Historical spirantization in Central Gur: earlier voiced stops shifted to fricatives including labiodentalfricative [v]. Also documented as *ɓ > b/v in some Gur languages.
PIE voiced labiovelar aspirate *gʷʰ → Latin /f/ or /v/ depending on position. This is a labiovelar shift, not directly g→v.
Velar weakening to glide, sometimes leading to labialized forms in related dialects.

sources: Sihler, A. L. (1995). New Comparative Grammar of Greek and Latin. Oxford University Press.
